text.title: Flux CD — Setup and Operation Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules including a `skip-this-methodology` rule. Each pairs a binary check with sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Each tenant MUST use the canonical Flux CRD set: GitRepository (source) → Kustomization (apply Kustomize) OR HelmRelease (apply Helm). Mixing apply mechanisms outside this set is rejected.
rule.rationale: Canonical CRDs are the reconciliation surface; ad-hoc Jobs that kubectl apply outside Flux destroy auditability.

rule.statement: Kustomization MUST set `spec.prune: true`. Without prune, removed manifests stay in the cluster (drift accumulation).
rule.rationale: Prune is what makes Git the source of truth; without it, Git ≠ cluster.

rule.statement: HelmRelease for production MUST be paired with ImagePolicy + ImageRepository + ImageUpdateAutomation when image-tag updates are expected. Manual chart version bumps for image-only changes are rejected.
rule.rationale: Manual bumps deviate from GitOps; image automation closes the loop end-to-end.

rule.statement: NotificationController MUST be configured to alert on reconcile failures + drift events to a team channel. Silent failures are rejected.
rule.rationale: Failures invisible until incidents; notifications shrink MTTR.

rule.statement: Multi-tenant setups MUST isolate via Namespace + RBAC + ServiceAccount per tenant. Cluster-wide tenants are rejected.
rule.rationale: Cluster-wide tenants enable cross-tenant blast radius; isolation is the only safe pattern.

rule.statement: Skip when (a) ArgoCD was chosen, (b) cluster k8s <1.25, or (c) single-team setup where UI tooling is the better fit.
rule.rationale: Tool fit dominates outcome; pushing Flux where ArgoCD fits creates resistance.
